title: World War I Tracker Update — Apr 19, 2026
summary: Four new events added for April 2026 covering WWI commemorations and historical discoveries. On April 1, descendants of the Choctaw Code Talkers gathered in Fort Worth, Texas, to unveil a historical marker honoring the WWI soldiers who pioneered Native American language code use. April 6 marked the 109th anniversary of the US declaration of war against Germany. On April 9, Canadian communities observed Vimy Ridge Day, and the Canadian War Museum acquired a century-old chair carved by a prisoner at the Spirit Lake WWI internment camp in Quebec. April 18 saw preparations underway across Australia and New Zealand for ANZAC Day 2026, marking the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings.

title: World War I Tracker Update — Sat Apr 18, 2026
summary: Communities across Australia and New Zealand prepared for ANZAC Day 2026 (April 25), marking the 111th anniversary of the Gallipoli landings on April 25, 1915.

title: World War I Tracker Update — Thu Apr 9, 2026
summary: Canada observed the 109th anniversary of the Battle of Vimy Ridge (April 9–12, 1917) with national and community-level ceremonies. A handcrafted wooden chair made in 1915 by a prisoner held at the Spirit Lake internment camp near Amos, Quebec, was acquired by the Canadian War Museum in Ottawa.

title: World War I Tracker Update — Mon Apr 6, 2026
summary: 109th Anniversary: United States Enters World War I (April 6, 1917).

title: World War I Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: Choctaw Code Talkers Honored with Fort Worth Historical Marker.
